---
title: "Catalogue and Planning"
subtitle: "`available_features()`, `plan_features()`, `extract_features()` - choose before running."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

The workflow catalogue is built from extractor specs. A plan chooses a route from available input modalities to requested feature IDs and records the extractor, input token, parameters, output schema, and opt-in status for each step.

## Invariants

- `available_features()` returns `FeatureCatalogEntry` rows derived from registered extractor specs.
- `plan_features()` returns a `FeaturePlan` unless `as_dataframe=True`.
- `FeaturePlan.to_recipe()` emits a recipe dictionary with a `features` list.
- Path inputs with image, audio, video, text, or NumPy suffixes are coerced to the matching stimulus or path contract.
- A stimulus named `movie` expands to audio and video modality planning.

## What it refuses

- Feature IDs that are not registered.
- Requests whose input modality cannot be routed.
- Optional or costly feature requests under the default budget.
- Placeholder features unless explicitly included.

## What it doesn't do

Planning does not run the extractor. Execution begins when the plan is converted to a recipe and passed through the recipe executor.

## See also

[List and plan features](../start/02-list-and-plan-features.qmd) · [Extract features](../start/03-extract-features.qmd) · [Evidence](../evidence/index.qmd)
